operating_zone.description: The geographical or operational area where the pipeline is located (e.g., a city, region, or designated zone). Typically found in the report header or project summary section.

pipeline_name.description: The official pipeline name as stated in the report or project documentation. Found in headers, title pages, or project summary sections.

project_manager.description: Full name (first and last) of the individual responsible for overseeing the project. Found in report headers, cover pages, or personnel sections.

field_integrity_supervisor.description: Full name of the supervisor overseeing pipeline integrity during inspections and maintenance. Found in report headers, cover pages, or personnel sections.

location.description: Specific geographic location of the pipeline, including city, state, landmarks, or coordinates. Found in headers or project summary sections.

pipe_diameter_mm.description: Nominal pipeline diameter in millimeters (mm). A positive numeric value found in engineering specs or project summary sections.

site_id.description: Unique identifier for the inspection site. May appear under labels: 'Site Name', 'Dig Number', or similar. Examples: 'GWD 16', 'Dig #1', 'JT 388', 'Dig #9 - 2011'. Extract as-is without modification.

inspection_start.description: Date (or date-time) when the inspection began. NOT a repair date. Output in ISO 8601 format when possible (e.g., 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ'). May also appear as 'Dec 09, 2009' or 'October 17, 2017'.

inspection_end.description: Date (or date-time) when the inspection concluded. NOT a repair date. Output in ISO 8601 format when possible (e.g., 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ'). May also appear as 'Dec 09, 2009' or 'October 17, 2017'.

client_project_engineer.description: Full name of the engineer overseeing the project on behalf of the client. Found in report headers, cover pages, or personnel sections. Include any titles or designations if present.

pipeline_system.description: Name or designation of the pipeline system (e.g., operational name or code). Found in headers or project summary sections.

max_allowable_operating_pressure_kPa.description: Maximum Allowable Operating Pressure (MAOP) of the pipeline in kilopascals (kPa). A non-negative integer from engineering specifications or project documentation.

repairs.description:
List of repair activities performed on the pipeline. Records are found in sections titled 'Sleeve Installation', 'Sleeve Repairs', 'New Coating', 'Recoat Information', 'Recoating', or similar. Each record describes one repair action (sleeve, recoat, etc.) with its location, type, and dates. If no repairs were performed, return null.

Important: the list includes only repair activities like recoating and similar, but must not include inspection or any other pipeline activities (like grinding, etc.)

Repair records may be presented as tables or free-text paragraphs. Supplementary comments (e.g., 'One 18inch petroline sleeve was installed...') may appear before or after tabular data and can contain additional repair details.

Key patterns:
- Sleeve repairs: Sleeve Type, Manufacturer/Brand, Serial Number, Axial start and Axial end.
- Recoat repairs (recoating): Coating Type/Brand, Application Method, Primer Type, Axial start and Axial end.

repair_start.description: Date (or date-time) when the repair began. This is a REPAIR date, NOT an inspection date. If only one date is given for the repair (e.g., 'Date Installed'), use it for both repair_start and repair_end. Output in ISO 8601 when possible.

repair_end.description: Date (or date-time) when the repair was completed or installed. This is a REPAIR date, NOT an inspection date. If only one date is given for the repair, use it for both repair_start and repair_end. Output in ISO 8601 when possible.

relative_repair_start.description:
Numeric axial position where the repair begins on the pipeline, relative to the reference girth weld. Can be negative. Found under labels: 'Axial Start (mm)', 'Coating Start (m)', 'Start Distance', or similar. 

Do NOT confuse with 'Axial Length of Coating' or 'Total Coating Length' — those are lengths, not start positions.
Do NOT convert values from one unit of measure to another. Keep the original values.

relative_repair_end.description:
Numeric axial position where the repair ends on the pipeline, relative to the reference girth weld. Can be negative. Found under labels: 'Axial End (mm)', 'Coating End (m)', 'End Distance', or similar. 

Do NOT confuse with 'Axial Length of Coating' or 'Total Coating Length' — those are lengths, not end positions. Appears adjacent to relative_repair_start.
Do NOT convert values from one unit of measure to another. Keep the original values.

reference_units.description:
Unit of measurement for relative_repair_start and relative_repair_end. Infer from the label context: 'Axial Start (mm)' → 'mm', 'Coating Start (m)' → 'm'. Common values: 'mm', 'm'.
If an unit of measure is not specified with the labels it also may come within the relative_repair_start or relative_repair_end values, for instance: -0.23 m, 3.70(m), 1.70 mm.

repair_type.description:
Type of repair performed. Infer from the section/table context if not explicitly stated per record (e.g., records under 'Recoat Information' → 'Recoat', records under 'Sleeve Installation' with Sleeve Type = Compression → 'Compression Sleeve').

Common values: Recoat, Compression Sleeve, Composite, Clock Spring, Petro Sleeve, Full Sole, Half Sole, Cut Out, Type "A" Reinforcement Sleeve, Type "B" Pressure Sleeve, Mechanical Clamp - Pressure Containing, Patch, Hot Tap, Deposition of Weld Metal, Remove By Grinding, Stand Off Sleeve, Girth Weld Sleeve, Temporary Clamp, Unknown, None, Other.

rgw.description: Reference Girth Weld (RGW) number — the girth weld used as the positional reference for this repair. Found under labels: 'Reference Girth Weld', 'Reference Point', 'Reference Point No.', or similar. Integer value (e.g., 950, 141). If not explicitly stated with the repair record, use the numeric portion of the site_id value.
